title: Session Validation Middleware on Every Request
description:
Every request on every host must validate the session using Stytch and produce a consistent auth context for downstream handlers.

## Validation Flow

1. Read `session_jwt` from cookie
2. Validate JWT locally using Stytch SDK (JWKS validation)
3. On expired JWT, refresh session via Stytch API
4. Yield auth context: `{organization_id, user_id, roles, tenant_slug, region}`
5. Unauthorized requests: redirect to auth start (browser) or return 401 (API)
6. Auth context is logged with correlation IDs

## Technical Approach

- Stytch SDK handles JWT validation locally (fast, no network call for valid tokens)
- JWT contains session claims from Stytch
- 5-minute JWT expiry requires periodic refresh via Stytch API
- No custom session storage needed — Stytch manages session state
acceptance_criteria: Middleware reads and validates session_jwt from cookie on every request
JWT validation uses Stytch JWKS (local, fast)
Expired JWTs trigger session refresh via Stytch API
Auth context includes organization_id, user_id, roles, tenant_slug, and region
Browser requests redirect to login when unauthorized
Auth context is logged with correlation IDs for traceability
middleware_changes: Global request middleware that validates session JWT via Stytch SDK, refreshes expired tokens, and produces a standardized auth context for all downstream handlers.
implementation_notes: JWT validation is local (JWKS cached) for most requests — only expired tokens require a network call to Stytch. The 5-minute JWT expiry means ~1 refresh call per 5 minutes per active user. Auth context must be consistent across all hosts (dedicated and pooled).
